name: Prime Meridian Bank
description_short: Prime Meridian Bank (now MIDFLORIDA Credit Union) offers personal and business checking, savings, loans, and mortgages with no monthly maintenance fees and local decision-making.
description_long:
Prime Meridian Bank operates as a community-focused financial institution serving the Florida area, with a primary office in Tallahassee. The bank recently merged with or transitioned to operating as MIDFLORIDA Credit Union, maintaining the Prime Meridian Bank brand while establishing membership through MIDFLORIDA. This transition represents a significant structural change in how the organization operates and what protections and benefits members receive. The institution emphasizes relationship-based banking with named relationship managers and a commitment to local community involvement through various charitable initiatives and sponsorships.

The bank offers comprehensive personal banking services including multiple checking account tiers (ePrime Personal Checking, Prime 360, Prime 50, Prime Advantage, and Prime BankOn), savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit, and health savings accounts. For business clients, Prime Meridian provides business checking accounts with no monthly maintenance charges and free initial checks, business savings products, high-yield money market accounts, and various loan products including small business loans, equipment loans, commercial real estate loans, and SBA loans. Personal loan offerings include consumer loans, personal lines of credit, auto loans, and home equity loans. The bank also provides home mortgage services with locally-made decisions and digital banking through online banking, mobile banking, and Zelle money transfer integration.

The bank distinguishes itself through emphasis on zero monthly maintenance fees on primary checking accounts, free initial check orders, no minimum balance requirements on certain accounts, and locally-made mortgage loan decisions. Their relationship manager model and emphasis on in-person service (offering fresh-baked cookies and coffee) reflects a community bank approach. Accessibility features including AudioEye toolbar and comprehensive fraud prevention tools (Positive Pay, ACH Fraud Filters, Secure Tokens) are highlighted. The recent merger with MIDFLORIDA means accounts are now NCUA-insured as a credit union, providing stronger regulatory protections than traditional bank deposit insurance.

However, potential members should note that the website content does not provide specific interest rates, APRs, loan terms, or detailed fee schedules for products beyond the stated no-maintenance-fee checking accounts. The ongoing transition to MIDFLORIDA branding creates uncertainty about service continuity and whether all advertised Prime Meridian products will maintain identical terms. Geographic service appears limited to Florida locations (Tallahassee and Crawfordville mentioned), which may restrict accessibility for non-local members. The statement that 'any account opened or renewed establishes membership with MIDFLORIDA' suggests existing customers may experience changes to membership terms. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal Checking (multiple tiers: ePrime, Prime 360, Prime 50, Prime Advantage, Prime BankOn)
Business Checking (Prime Business, Small Business, Regular, NOW accounts)
Personal Savings Accounts and Money Market Accounts
Business Savings and High-Yield Business Money Market
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) for personal and business
Personal Loans (consumer loans, personal lines of credit, auto loans, home equity loans)
Business Loans (small business, equipment, commercial real estate, SBA loans, business lines of credit)
Home Mortgage lending with local decisions
Online Banking and Mobile Banking with Mobile Deposit
Bill Pay and Zelle money transfer
Treasury Management Services (ACH Origination, Remote Deposit, Online Cash Management)
Fraud Prevention Services (Positive Pay, ACH Fraud Filters, Secure Tokens)
pros: No monthly maintenance charges on Prime Business Checking and ePrime Personal Checking accounts
First 50 checks or debits free monthly on business checking, plus free first check order on personal accounts
Mortgage decisions made locally rather than at remote processing centers
NCUA insurance protection as a credit union member (more comprehensive than standard FDIC)
Multiple checking account options tailored to different customer profiles (students, seniors, basic banking)
Comprehensive digital banking including mobile deposit, online bill pay, and Zelle integration
Named relationship managers for personalized service (Tyler Harris, Taylor Joyner, Heather Benton listed)
Fraud prevention tools including Positive Pay, ACH Fraud Filters, and Secure Tokens
cons: Website provides no specific interest rates, APRs, or detailed fee schedules for loan and savings products
Limited geographic footprint (appears to serve only Florida, specifically Tallahassee and Crawfordville areas)
Ongoing merger/rebranding transition to MIDFLORIDA creates uncertainty about service continuity and whether existing account terms will change
No information about minimum deposits required for savings accounts, CDs, or money market accounts
Website contains browser security warnings and accessibility toolbar prompts that create a less polished user experience
best_for: Florida-based small business owners seeking no-fee checking with locally-made lending decisions
Personal customers in the Tallahassee area wanting relationship-based banking with no monthly maintenance fees
Homebuyers seeking mortgage lending with local decision-making rather than remote processing
Credit union members who value NCUA insurance protections and community involvement emphasis
diagnosis: Prime Meridian Bank is best for Florida-based individuals and small businesses seeking a community bank experience with no-fee checking accounts and locally-made decisions, particularly those in the Tallahassee area. The main caveat is that the recent transition to operating as MIDFLORIDA Credit Union creates uncertainty about service continuity, and the website lacks specific pricing details (interest rates, APRs, fees) needed to compare offerings against competitors; prospective customers should contact the bank directly for current product terms.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
